headline: Government pressure on Anthropic dominates the week as its cybersecurity models are pulled and a jailbreak dispute plays out in public.
body: The Trump administration forced Anthropic to withdraw its cybersecurity models, an action one account frames as political retaliation rather than a genuine security failure, while Katie Moussouris reviewed the White House report on the Fable jailbreak and characterized the model behavior as working as intended for cyberdefense. Anthropic also faces a separate lawsuit alleging false advertising over Claude Max subscription limits. On the technique side, memory eviction and skill evolution lead at peak ratio, while advantage weighting, language model, tenant isolation, conformal prediction, Mahalanobis distance, model pruning, and data selection register as fresh breakouts. Infrastructure strain surfaced as Microsoft turned to AWS to relieve a GitHub AI capacity crunch, and AWS WAF added a mechanism for content owners to charge AI bots for access.

cited_items.title: Microsoft turns to AWS as GitHub faces AI capacity crunch
Anthropic Sued over Alleged False Advertising on Claude Max Subscription Limits
The White House Is Ratcheting Up Its War Against Anthropic
AWS WAF now lets content owners charge AI bots for access
The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak
